name: The Italian Game as White
description: A White repertoire for the Italian Game, drilled one position at a time: every card is a position with exactly one book move to find. Built from 3,000,000 rated games (Lichess database 2026-06, ratings 1600-2200): opponent replies branch by how often they are actually played, covering the most common 90% at each turn, so frequent lines run deep and rare sidelines stay shallow. 72 positions.

fields.front: What is the idea of the Italian Game?
fields.back: Develop fast and point the c4 bishop at f7, Black's weakest square; then either build the center slowly with c3/d3 or open fire with d4 and the gambits
fields.extra: The oldest opening plan in chess: knight out, bishop to its most aggressive safe square, castle. Everything Black does gets measured against the threats to f7 and White's d4 break.
